StatusNote: The 2005-06 season currently includes the one confirmed high-school meet visible in the public archive: the April 29, 2006 SCISA state championships.
RecapParagraphs: The 2005-06 season now opens the St. Andrew's track archive with the earliest confirmed SCISA postseason snapshot currently visible in the public results: the April 29, 2006 state championships at Heathwood Hall in Columbia.

The public file is small, but it shows both sides of the program. On the girls side, Ann Kingston reached the 100 hurdles final and also appeared in the 100 dash, Sarah Lundgren ran the 400, and Grace Wilkowski logged throws marks in both shot put and discus. On the boys side, Javier Gutierrez represented St. Andrew's in the long jump.

None of those marks reached the scoring positions, and St. Andrew's did not appear in the published team standings. Even so, the meet gives the track archive one more verified SCISA season at the front end of the timeline.
HighlightNotes: Ann Kingston placed 7th in the girls 100-meter hurdles at the 2006 SCISA Championships in 18.62.
Grace Wilkowski placed 7th in the girls shot put at 27-08.50 and also competed in the discus.
Javier Gutierrez placed 10th in the boys long jump at 18-01.00.
The meet pushes the public track archive back to the 2005-06 school year.

StatusNote: The 2006-07 season currently includes the one confirmed high-school meet visible in the public archive: the April 28, 2007 SCISA state championships.
RecapParagraphs: The 2006-07 season adds the next early SCISA postseason snapshot in the St. Andrew's track archive, built around the April 28, 2007 state championships at Heathwood Hall in Columbia.

The public file is extremely small for St. Andrew's. The only visible result is the boys 4x800 relay, which finished seventh in 10:13.63. No St. Andrew's scoring marks appeared in the boys raw page, and the team was not listed in the published boys standings.

The linked girls raw page did not show any St. Andrew's entries, so the season reads as a narrow relay-only postseason snapshot rather than a full team record. Even so, it gives the track archive one more confirmed early SCISA chapter.
HighlightNotes: The boys 4x800 relay finished 7th at the 2007 SCISA Championships in 10:13.63.
No St. Andrew's scoring finishes were visible in the public boys raw file.
No St. Andrew's girls entries were visible in the linked public girls raw file.
The season preserves a relay-only postseason snapshot from the early SCISA years.

StatusNote: The 2008-09 season currently includes the one confirmed high-school meet visible in the public archive: the May 2, 2009 SCISA state championships.
RecapParagraphs: The 2008-09 season adds the next early SCISA postseason snapshot in the St. Andrew's track archive, built around the May 2, 2009 state championships at Heathwood Hall in Columbia.

The file is compact and boys-only, but it still preserves a clear team identity. Ikaika Kapu won the boys shot put, Lee Smith followed in second to give St. Andrew's a one-two finish in the event, and those throws points carried the team to fourth in the Class A & AA boys standings with eight total points.

Outside the throws ring, Jay Jenkins reached the boys 3200 state final and the boys 4x100 relay appears with Danny Kirkconnell, Jon Hope, Harry Jozefczyk, and Don Tomberlin before a disqualification wiped out the result. No St. Andrew's girls entries were visible in the public Class A & AA girls raw file, so the season reads as a narrow but meaningful boys postseason snapshot.
HighlightNotes: Ikaika Kapu won the boys shot put at the 2009 SCISA Championships with a 42-03.00.
Lee Smith finished 2nd in the boys shot put at 40-10.00 to complete a St. Andrew's one-two finish.
Jay Jenkins reached the boys 3200 state final and placed 5th in 11:30.46.
The boys team finished 4th in Class A & AA with eight points.

StatusNote: The 2009-10 season currently includes the one confirmed high-school meet visible in the public archive: the May 1, 2010 SCISA state championships.
RecapParagraphs: The 2009-10 season adds the next early SCISA postseason snapshot in the St. Andrew's track archive, built around the May 1, 2010 state championships at Heathwood Hall in Columbia.

The boys side supplied most of the points. Andrew Smith led the way by winning shot put and discus while also taking second in high jump and long jump, Grant Little scored in both short sprints, the boys 4x400 relay placed second, and Jay Jenkins added a third-place finish in the 1600. Together those results pushed St. Andrew's to ninth in the boys team standings with 22 points.

The girls file is smaller but still strong. Rachel Huncke placed second in the girls 400, Ciara Brown scored in both jumps and won the girls long jump, Maggie Solitario placed second in the girls discus, and Addison Randolph reached the girls 800 final. The girls totaled 16 points and finished 10th in the team standings.
HighlightNotes: Andrew Smith won the boys shot put at 42-07.00 and the boys discus at 121-10 at the 2010 SCISA Championships.
Smith also added runner-up finishes in boys high jump and boys long jump.
Ciara Brown won the girls long jump at 14-11.75 and also scored in the girls high jump.
Rachel Huncke placed 2nd in the girls 400, while Grant Little scored in both the boys 100 and 200.

StatusNote: The 2010-11 season currently includes the one confirmed high-school meet visible in the public archive: the May 7, 2011 SCISA state championships.
RecapParagraphs: The 2010-11 season adds the next early SCISA postseason snapshot in the St. Andrew's track archive, built around the May 7, 2011 state championships at Wilson Hall in Sumter.

The boys side carried the biggest headline. Andrew Smith won four Class A & AA state titles by taking the boys 100, boys 200, shot put, and discus, while Danny Kirkconnell added sprint points in both short dashes and Tucker Whitton, Jay Jenkins, and the relay groups pushed the boys to ninth in the team standings with 33 points.

The girls file also shows a real team presence instead of a one-athlete cameo. Rachel Huncke placed second in both the girls 100 and 400, Klara Hodges finished third in the girls 800 and 1600, Maggie Solitario won the girls shot put, and the girls 4x100 relay took second with Huncke, Rebecca Suh, Halen Waters, and Hodges. Those results lifted St. Andrew's to 11th in the girls standings with 16 points.
HighlightNotes: Andrew Smith won the boys 100 and 200 at the 2011 SCISA Championships in 11.46 and 23.38.
Smith also swept the boys throws by winning shot put at 43-05.50 and discus at 125-02.
Rachel Huncke placed 2nd in both the girls 100 and girls 400, then helped the girls 4x100 relay finish 2nd.
Klara Hodges placed 3rd in both the girls 800 and 1600, and Maggie Solitario won the girls shot put.

StatusNote: The 2011-12 season currently includes the one confirmed high-school meet visible in the public archive: the May 5, 2012 SCISA state championships.
RecapParagraphs: The 2011-12 season adds the next early SCISA postseason snapshot in the St. Andrew's track archive, built around the May 5, 2012 state championships at Porter-Gaud in Charleston.

The girls side supplied nearly all of the scoring. Rebecca Suh placed second in both the girls 100 and 200, Morgan Bennet tied for second in the high jump, and Morgan Randolph added a fourth-place finish in the 800 plus a third-place finish in the 1600. Those results pushed St. Andrew's to 15th in the girls team standings with nine points.

The boys file is smaller but still meaningful. Jay Jenkins finished fourth in the boys 1600 and second in the 3200 to account for the team's three points and a 17th-place finish in the boys standings. The girls 4x100 relay also appears in the archive with Rachel Huncke, Victoria McLaren, Rebecca Suh, and Morgan Bennet before a disqualification kept it off the final podium.
HighlightNotes: Rebecca Suh placed 2nd in the girls 100 at the 2012 SCISA Championships in 13.25 and 2nd again in the girls 200 in 27.93.
Morgan Bennet tied for 2nd in the girls high jump at 4-04.00.
Morgan Randolph placed 3rd in the girls 1600 and 4th in the girls 800 at state.
Jay Jenkins placed 2nd in the boys 3200 in 10:36.16 and also finished 4th in the boys 1600.

StatusNote: The 2013-14 season currently includes the one confirmed high-school meet visible in the public archive: the May 3, 2014 SCISA state championships.
RecapParagraphs: The 2013-14 season provides the next SCISA postseason snapshot in the St. Andrew's track archive, built around the May 3, 2014 championships at Heathwood Hall.

The girls side supplied the biggest results. Rebecca Suh won the girls Class A & AA 200, the girls 4x100 relay won in 53.02, and the girls 4x400 relay followed with another title in 4:18.93. Kaylin Rogers added a third-place finish in the girls 100 hurdles, and Morgan Randolph placed third in the girls 800.

The boys file is smaller, with Malcolm Williams-Green currently showing as the lone confirmed St. Andrew's boys result after placing fifth in the Class A & AA 100. The archived live-results pages also list St. Andrew's entries in the girls 100, but that event page only preserved the meet-program heat sheet rather than final marks.
HighlightNotes: Rebecca Suh won the girls 200 at the 2014 SCISA Championships in 26.85.
The girls 4x100 relay won the Class A & AA state title in 53.02 with Marie Solitario, Georgia Miller, Rebecca Suh, and Morgan Bennet.
The girls 4x400 relay won the Class A & AA state title in 4:18.93 with Georgia Miller, Marie Solitario, Morgan Bennet, and Morgan Randolph.
Kaylin Rogers and Morgan Randolph added third-place state finishes in the girls 100 hurdles and girls 800.

StatusNote: The 2014-15 season now includes two verified St. Andrew's meets: the April 24 RCA A and AA SCISA Regional Qualifier and the May 2 SCISA state championships.
RecapParagraphs: The 2014-15 season now reaches beyond a one-meet state snapshot, with the April 24 RCA A and AA SCISA Regional Qualifier adding a much fuller picture of the St. Andrew's roster before the May 2 SCISA championships.

Rebecca Suh still stands at the center of the season. She won the girls 100 at the regional qualifier, the girls 4x100 and 4x400 relays both won with the St. Andrew's sprint group, and then Suh returned to place third in the 100 and fourth in the 200 at state while both relays finished second.

The boys side also becomes more substantial with the qualifier added. Malcolm Williams-Green won the boys 100 and placed second in the 200 at region before scoring again at state, while Daniel Chisner, Lucas Zhang, John Ashton, Jacob Dixon, and J.D. Morgan give the season distance and throws depth that was previously hidden.
HighlightNotes: Rebecca Suh won the girls 100 at the 2015 RCA A and AA SCISA Regional Qualifier, and the girls 4x100 and 4x400 relays also won that meet.
At the 2015 SCISA state championships, Rebecca Suh placed 3rd in the girls 100 and 4th in the 200, while both girls relays finished 2nd.
Malcolm Williams-Green won the boys 100 and placed 2nd in the boys 200 at the regional qualifier before scoring again at state.
Brea Yates, Lucas Zhang, Daniel Chisner, and the boys throws group broaden the season beyond the headline sprint results.

StatusNote: The 2015-16 season now includes two verified St. Andrew's regular-season meets, from the March 9 Porter-Gaud meet through the April 20 Porter-Gaud Home Meet 3. No public postseason file has been attached yet.
RecapParagraphs: The 2015-16 season is now the earliest St. Andrew's spring in the site with multiple regular-season meets instead of a postseason-only snapshot.

The results show a small but balanced roster. Marie Solitario supplied girls 400 marks, Corinne Standing and Phoebe Mayo gave the team distance depth, the girls 4x800 relay appears in the March opener, and Brea Yates anchored the throws group. On the boys side, Malcolm Williams-Green and Cole Becker handled the sprints while Ben Mayo, Connor Dodge, and John Ashton gave the season range from the 400 through the 3200.
No public postseason file is attached yet, so the year reads as a developmental regular-season chapter that helps bridge the earliest SCISA seasons to the fuller 2016-17 and 2017-18 archives.
HighlightNotes: Marie Solitario opened the loaded season with a 1:09.89 girls 400 at the March 9 Porter-Gaud meet.
John Ashton posted boys 3200 marks of 14:12.03 and 14:03.37 across the two loaded 2016 meets.
Cole Becker reached the boys sprint finals in both the 100 and 200 at the April 20 Porter-Gaud Home Meet 3.
The girls 4x800 relay ran 12:09.12 at the March 9 Porter-Gaud meet.

StatusNote: The 2016-17 season now includes five verified meets, from four regular-season stops through the May 6 SCISA state championships.
RecapParagraphs: The 2016-17 season now reads as a fuller SCISA spring rather than a one-meet state snapshot, with four regular-season results loaded before the May 6 SCISA championships.

Malcolm Williams-Green became the clearest sprint headline. He won the boys 100 at the Orangeburg Prep meet, placed second in the 200 there and again at Porter Gaud Home Meet #3, and then added a sixth-place finish in the 200 at state. Around him, Connor Dodge gave the team a steady hurdles-and-jumps presence, while Grant Viands and Ben Bingley anchored the middle-distance group.

Jack Hagan still closes the year with the biggest postseason result by placing third in the boys shot put at state, but the added March and April meets now show much more of the roster and the week-to-week shape of the season.
HighlightNotes: The spring now includes four regular-season meets at Porter Gaud and Orangeburg before the 2017 SCISA state championships.
Malcolm Williams-Green won the boys 100 at Orangeburg Prep, later placed 2nd in the boys 200 at Porter Gaud Home Meet #3, and then scored in the boys 200 at state.
Connor Dodge supplied points in the boys 400 hurdles and long jump, while Grant Viands and Ben Bingley anchored the middle-distance group.
Jack Hagan capped the year with a 3rd-place finish in boys shot put at the SCISA championships.

StatusNote: The 2017-18 season now includes four verified meets, from three Porter Gaud regular-season meets through the May 5 SCISA championships.
RecapParagraphs: The 2017-18 season now extends well beyond a one-row state file, with three Porter Gaud regular-season meets loaded ahead of the May 5 SCISA championships.

Jack Hagan remains the headline performer. He placed second in shot put at Porter Gaud Home Meets #1, #2, and #3 before winning the SCISA state title at 43-08.50. Around that throws core, Connor Dodge gave the boys side hurdles and long-jump versatility, while Grant Viands, Harrison Soldan, and Cade Raeburn added middle-distance and 400-meter depth.

The girls file is also clearer with the regular season attached. Phoebe Mayo led the distance group, Corinne Standing contributed sprint-jump marks, and Erin Horesh and Elina Aziz appear in the longer races. The added meets turn the season from a single championship memory into a recognizable team year.
HighlightNotes: Jack Hagan placed 2nd in shot put at Porter Gaud Home Meets #1, #2, and #3 before winning the 2018 SCISA state title at 43-08.50.
Phoebe Mayo led the girls distance group through the 2018 regular season, while Connor Dodge and Grant Viands added boys hurdles, jumps, and middle-distance depth.
Harrison Soldan and Cade Raeburn supplied regular-season 400-meter marks, and Corinne Standing contributed girls sprint-jump results.
The state meet still preserves the outright championship mark, but the added Porter Gaud files now give the season a much fuller team picture.

StatusNote: The 2018-19 season currently includes the one confirmed high-school meet still in the archive: the April 25-27, 2019 GISA state championship.
RecapParagraphs: The 2018-19 season now appears in the track archive as a single-meet postseason snapshot after the April 2 event was identified as middle school only and removed from the varsity record. That leaves the GISA state championship as the lone confirmed high-school meet currently attached to the season.

Even with just one meet, the state results still give the season a recognizable shape. Phoebe Mayo represented the girls distance group in the 800 and 1600, while the boys side brought distance entries plus a throws group that scored in both shot put and discus.

Jack Hagan delivered the top point totals for St. Andrew's at state by placing fourth in shot put and fourth in discus, and the rest of the loaded results preserve a small but varied roster across distance and field events.
HighlightNotes: Jack Hagan placed 4th in both boys shot put and boys discus at the 2019 GISA state championship.
Phoebe Mayo represented St. Andrew's in the girls 800 and 1600 at state.
Tate Dixon added a scoring finish in boys shot put with 5th place.
Victor Balboni, Aiden Luther, and Erickson Rasplica gave the boys distance group three separate state entries.

StatusNote: The 2020-21 season now includes the two confirmed high-school postseason meets visible in the archived schedule: the April 21 GISA Region 2AAA Championship and the April 29-May 1 GISA state championship.
RecapParagraphs: The 2020-21 season reads as a compact postseason chapter in the track archive, with St. Andrew's public results appearing only at the GISA Region 2AAA Championship and the GISA state meet. Even with just those two meets, the season still shows a clear profile: girls distance strength led by Phoebe Mayo, supporting sprint and throws entries, and a small boys group competing in distance and discus.

Mayo supplied the season's biggest result by winning the girls 3200 at region and then returning to place fifth in the same event at state. She also placed third in the region 1600 and finished 11th at state, giving the season page a strong through-line from regional success into state-level competition.

Around that distance core, Morgan Rule scored in girls shot put at region, Ella McWhorter added sprint entries, and Victor Balboni, Ajay Watson, and Ben Bingley gave the boys side distance, sprint, and discus results. It is a short season in the archive, but it still adds another early track year with identifiable postseason performances.
HighlightNotes: Phoebe Mayo won the girls 3200 at the 2021 GISA Region 2AAA Championship in 14:11.36 and followed it with a 5th-place finish at the GISA state meet.
Mayo also placed 3rd in the girls 1600 at region and competed in the same event at state.
Morgan Rule scored in girls shot put at the region championship with a 4th-place finish.
Victor Balboni gave the boys side region entries in both the 1600 and 3200.

StatusNote: The 2021-22 season now includes all four confirmed high-school meets from the archived schedule, from the March 2 Savannah Country Day home meet through the April 22-23 GISA state championships.
RecapParagraphs: The 2021-22 season now gives the track archive another full four-meet year, beginning with two Savannah Country Day home meets and closing with the GISA region and state championship meets. Even in a compact schedule, the results show a team built around sprint points, a few dependable middle-distance efforts, and girls throws production that carried into championship season.

Anthony Kusilka supplied the biggest postseason breakthrough by winning the boys 400 at the Region 2AAA championships and then returning to score again with a fourth-place finish in the state final. Andrew Futch added speed across the spring with scoring runs in the short sprints, a region bronze in the 100, and state-meet appearances in the 100 and long jump.

On the girls side, Morgan Rule anchored the throws group by scoring in shot put at both region and state, while Gray Holmes reached the state meet in the 800 after a third-place region finish. The season page also preserves the boys 4x100 and 4x400 relay groups from both championship meets, giving the 2021-22 archive a clearer picture of the team beyond individual entries.
HighlightNotes: Anthony Kusilka won the boys 400 at the 2022 GISA Region 2AAA meet in 54.09 and followed it with a 53.29 for 4th at the GISA state championships.
Morgan Rule scored in girls shot put at both championship meets, placing 4th at region and 7th at state.
Gray Holmes advanced from a 3rd-place finish in the girls 800 at region to a state-meet berth in the same event.
The boys 4x100 and 4x400 relays both reached the state meet after placing 2nd and 4th, respectively, at the region championships.

StatusNote: The 2022-23 season now includes four verified meets, from the two Savannah Country Day regular-season meets through the Region 2AAA and GIAA state championship meets.
RecapParagraphs: The 2022-23 season now gives the track archive a full four-meet year to work from, starting with two Savannah Country Day regular-season meets and ending with region and state championship competition. Even in a short schedule, the loaded results show a balanced team with sprint speed, developing distance depth, and a girls throws group that scored when the meets mattered most.

Grace Dare emerged as one of the season's clearest postseason performers by placing second in both the girls 1600 and 3200 at the Region 2AAA championships before moving on to the state meet in those same distance events. On the boys side, Andrew Futch supplied the biggest sprint points, finishing runner-up in both the 100 and 200 at region and then advancing to state in the short sprints and long jump.

The season page also captures a broad supporting cast around those headline results. Morgan Rule scored in the girls shot put at region and qualified for state, the girls 4x800 relay reached both region and state, and Nick Brown, Ian Given, Ryder Small, Tripp Jackson, and the young throws group all helped shape the identity of the team.
HighlightNotes: Grace Dare placed 2nd in both the girls 1600 and 3200 at the 2023 Region 2AAA championships, then qualified on to the GIAA state meet in both events.
Andrew Futch finished 2nd in both the boys 100 and 200 at region and later competed at state in the 100, 200, and long jump.
Morgan Rule scored with a 3rd-place finish in girls shot put at region and went on to place 11th at the state meet.
The girls 4x800 relay reached both region and state, with the region lineup of Maren Wrieden, Sydney Mims, Gray Holmes, and Grace Dare posted in the public results.

StatusNote: The 2023-24 season currently includes the two postseason meets St. Andrew's contested in the public archive: the April 17, 2024 GIAA Region 2AAA Championships and the April 25-27, 2024 GIAA AAA State Meet.
RecapParagraphs: The 2023-24 track season currently appears in the public archive as a short postseason run, with St. Andrew's results loaded from the Region 2AAA championships and the GIAA AAA state meet. Even with just those two meets, the file already shows the shape of the team clearly: throws points, distance entries, and sprint-relay depth on the boys side.

Tripp Jackson delivered the headline region result by winning the boys shot put and placing fourth in discus, while Nick Brown and Ryder Small gave the team scoring and state-level presence in the distance races. Ellie Waldron also scored in the girls shot put at region before advancing to the state meet.

Because the loaded season is concentrated in championship competition, the 2023-24 page reads as a postseason snapshot rather than a full week-to-week schedule. It still adds an important earlier chapter to the track archive and pushes the section back one more school year.
HighlightNotes: Tripp Jackson won the boys shot put at the Region 2AAA championships with a 43-1.50 and also placed 4th in discus.
Nick Brown placed 2nd in the boys 3200 at region and returned to the GIAA AAA state meet in the same event.
Ellie Waldron scored in the girls shot put at region and later competed at state.
The boys 4x100, 4x400, and 4x800 relays all appear in the region results, with the 4x100 relay placing 4th.

StatusNote: The 2024-25 season currently includes five verified St. Andrew's meets, ending with the April 16, 2025 region championships and the May 1-3, 2025 state championships.
StateMeetLocation: Strong Rock Christian School, Locust Grove
RecapParagraphs: The 2024-25 season is the first St. Andrew's track year currently loaded meet-by-meet in the site, and it already shows the identity of the program clearly: distance depth, developing relay chemistry, and throws groups that kept adding points as the spring moved along.

Grace Dare opened the year with a runner-up finish in the girls 800, Lily McAtee advanced to the state meet in the 200, and Ellie Waldron gave the girls side a steady presence in the throws. On the boys side, Miles Cummings kept improving in the shot put, and the 4x800 relay turned into one of the season's clearest progression stories.

By postseason, the archive shows St. Andrew's athletes competing in distance races, sprints, jumps, throws, and relays at both region and state. That gives the section a strong first season page and a useful base for future event-group and championship-history additions.
HighlightNotes: Grace Dare placed 2nd in the girls 800 meters with a 2:34.51 at Savannah Country Day School Varsity Meet #2.
Miles Cummings closed the season with a 42-6.25 shot put at the GIAA state meet.
The boys 4x800 relay improved from 9:37.88 at the first loaded meet to 9:10.25 at state.
State-meet entries are loaded for Lily McAtee, Ryder Small, Miles Cummings, Ellie Waldron, Grace Dare, and the boys 4x800 relay.

StatusNote: As of April 20, 2026, the season meet list below has been confirmed from the current team schedule. The April 15, 2026 Savannah Country Day School Varsity Meet #6 has been intentionally omitted because St. Andrew's did not participate.
StateMeetLocation: Strong Rock Christian School, Locust Grove
RecapParagraphs: The 2025-26 season file is still growing, but the first four completed meets already show more event coverage across both the girls and boys groups. The section now has enough data to track weekly progress instead of only listing a schedule.

Aubri Rutledge has supplied the headline sprint marks so far on the girls side, while Janiyah Johnson has anchored the throws with strong discus and shot put performances. The distance group has also stayed active every week, with Lane Mandel and Nick Brown providing the most visible middle-distance and distance marks in the current file.

On the boys side, the 4x100 relay is already down to 45.70, Luke Nelson has paired sprint speed with a strong discus mark, and Josiah Bowers has contributed in both the high jump and triple jump. With region and state still ahead on the calendar, this page is designed to keep filling in as postseason results are added.
HighlightNotes: Aubri Rutledge has posted the team's best girls sprint marks so far with a 13.32 in the 100 and a 27.09 in the 200.
The boys 4x100 relay has already reached 45.70 through the first four completed meets.
Lane Mandel's 13:08.48 in the 3200 and Nick Brown's 5:11.95 in the 1600 lead the loaded distance results.
Region and state dates are already on the timeline, so the meet-by-meet archive can keep growing through postseason.
